exceptions.reason: aria-haspopup="menu" is set on the wrapping <span> around `trigger`, not on the trigger element itself (e.g. the IconButton's own <button>). A screen reader focused on the actual interactive element never sees it, since ARIA states on an ancestor are not inherited by the focused descendant.
Same placement problem as roles.haspopup: aria-expanded lives on the wrapping <span>, not on the focusable trigger, so it is invisible to assistive tech at the point where it is needed.
No code moves focus into the menu when it opens. DOM focus stays wherever it already was -- normally the trigger -- so the pattern's 'first menu item' focus never happens.
Enter does open the menu, because the trigger's native click bubbles to the wrapping span's onClick, but focus is never moved to the first menu item afterward -- only half of this requirement is met.
Same as keyboard.Enter: Space opens the menu via the same bubbled click, but focus never moves to the first item.

Escape does close the menu (a document keydown listener calls setOpen(false)), but nothing explicitly restores focus to the trigger button afterward -- if a user had tabbed into a menu item, focus is left nowhere in particular once the menu unmounts.
